prompt:
Tech-debt window finally opened, so here is the ticket I have been sitting on for a year. depotops.ps1 is the spare-parts depot toolbox: CSV loading, row validation, ordinal sorting, and three commands (report / shortfall / audit) all tangled into one ~300-line script. It works, and the crew runs it every morning, which is exactly why nobody has dared touch it. The problem: the fleet dashboard team wants the same logic from THEIR scripts, and today their only option is shelling out to the CLI and screen-scraping stdout.

Split it into a real module, observable behavior frozen:

- `DepotOps.psm1` plus a `DepotOps.psd1` manifest, both in the repo root. The manifest is the import surface -- `Import-Module ./DepotOps.psd1` from the workspace root must just work, and `FunctionsToExport` must list EXACTLY the five public functions below (helpers stay module-private; a wildcard export that leaks helpers fails review).
- Public API. Module functions never print and never call exit; failures are thrown terminating errors, and the messages carry no 'depotops:' prefix -- prefixes and exit codes are CLI concerns:
  - `Import-DepotStock -Path <file>` -> typed records with Sku, Desc, Qty, Min (Qty/Min are [int]). Throws '<leaf>: bad row <n>' (n = 1-based physical line, header is line 1) on a row with empty sku or non-integer qty/min; throws 'file not found: <path>' when the file is absent.
  - `Import-DepotOrders -Path <file>` -> records with Order, Sku, Qty ([int]); same bad-row and missing-file contract.
  - `Get-DepotReport -Stock <records>` -> objects with Sku, Qty, Min, Status ('LOW' when qty is strictly below min, else 'ok'), sorted ordinal by Sku.
  - `Get-DepotShortfall -Stock <records> -Orders <records>` -> objects with Sku and Need ([int]), the same math the CLI does today; throws 'unknown sku in orders: <sku>' naming the ordinal-first offender.
  - `Get-DepotFindings -Stock <records> [-Orders <records>]` -> finding objects with Kind and Subject, sorted ordinal by the rendered '<Kind> <Subject>' string.
- depotops.ps1 stays, as a thin consumer of the module. Its command surface is FROZEN: same stdout bytes, same stderr messages, same exit codes (0 / 64 / 65 / 66) for every invocation that works today.

Acceptance is `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_depotops.ps1` from the workspace root -- it pins the old CLI byte-for-byte and exercises the new module surface. The test file is off limits.
notes: 6P7 item 1: monolith -> module refactor. files/ ships a working ~270-line depotops.ps1 monolith (all CLI pins pass at baseline); verify fails only because DepotOps.psd1/.psm1 do not exist yet. PowerShell-native surface: .psd1 manifest authoring with FunctionsToExport as the export gate (harness pins ExportedFunctions == exactly the 5 names AND Get-Command -Module count == 5, so 'FunctionsToExport = *' plus helper functions fails), module-scoped private helpers, thrown-terminating-error API vs CLI exit-code mapping (64/65/66). Ordinal discipline throughout (List.Sort with CompareOrdinal comparison; ordinal HashSet/Dictionary so sku case never folds). Differentiation: ps-svcplan/ps-acctaudit are build seeds with flat scripts; no existing ps seed touches module manifests; py-clientsplit/ts-mailroster are the wave-4 module-split analogs but the manifest+FunctionsToExport mechanics have no bash/py analog (the catalog's stated point).
